notes:
UI text fix, taskbar icon switched from Pillow-Lanczos raster downscale to true vector rasterization, and a bag of release-pipeline reliability fixes. The v1.0.48 publish ALSO finally serves correctly on `cjml34483/ASA-releases` because v1.0.49's manifest commit busts Fastly's `raw.githubusercontent.com` cache (which had been serving v1.0.47 for the canonical repo despite the v1.0.48 commit having landed on `main` cleanly).

### Taskbar icon: vector pipeline replaces Pillow-Lanczos raster downscale

v1.0.48's icon pipeline (`scripts/regen_icon.py`) isolated the green S from a 1200├Ś426 raster wordmark master via green-channel dominance, padded 10%, then downscaled to each ICO frame size (16/20/24/30/32/36/40/48/64/96/128/256) with multi-step Lanczos + a mild UnsharpMask on frames Ōēż48 px. That works passably at Ōēź48 px and noticeably softer than vector-native Windows shipping icons (Settings, Photos, Edge) at 16/20/24/32 px ŌĆö those apps render their icons from vector geometry at each exact pixel size, which gives clean polygon edges that the resampling-from-raster approach can't match.

v1.0.49 swaps to a vector pipeline:
- New SVG master at `branding/svg/asa-glyph-s.svg` ŌĆö three face panels + two fold trapezoids, 5 polygons total, viewBox 0 0 280 280. Pre-v1.0.36 there was an analogous SVG at the same path but with phosphor-green (`#5FFF7E`) and a brighter highlight strip from a different brand era. v1.0.49 keeps the same geometry concept but uses the current Copilot wordmark's actual greens, sampled directly from `static/branding/wordmark-on-dark@3x.png` by point-sampling face/fold region centers ŌĆö face `#37a836`, fold `#2d8f33`. The pre-v1.0.36 highlight strips are dropped: at small sizes a 6-px highlight collapses to a single-row antialiasing artifact, and the current Copilot artwork doesn't have one.
- `scripts/regen_icon.py` rewritten to render the SVG via `resvg-py` (Rust-backed SVG rasterizer, exposed through a self-contained Python package ŌĆö important on this Windows dev machine where libcairo isn't installed and `cairosvg` therefore won't load). resvg rasterizes at the target pixel grid in one shot with gamma-correct subpixel antialiasing on polygon edges, no resampling step.
- Padding: 10% on each side, applied via an outer SVG that translates+scales the master into a centered (1 - 2┬Ępad)┬Ętarget box. Means the visible glyph never touches the icon's outer pixel ring at 16 px even after Windows composites with its 1-px alpha bleed.
- ICO is still written with PNG-encoded frames (legal since Vista; bypasses Pillow's BMP+AND-mask ICO writer which would re-encode and could drop subpixel detail).

Honest caveat: at 16/20 px the *design itself* limits legibility ŌĆö three 80-unit bars with 20-unit gaps in a 280-unit canvas means gap height Ōēł 1 px after padding. The vector path produces cleaner pixel edges than Lanczos there, but doesn't transform the design into Settings-icon visual density. Future iterations could ship a simplified hand-tuned sprite for Ōēż24 px and the vector path for Ōēź32 px.

`scripts/regen_icon.py --compare` writes side-by-side comparison PNGs at 16/32/48 px into `scripts/icon-compare/` (4├Ś nearest-neighbor upscale on a checkerboard so subpixel detail is visible). These are dev-only artifacts; not bundled.

### "MT5 Connection" / "Bridge" ŌåÆ "Connections"

Sidebar nav button label, page heading, error-modal copy, and the onboarding broker-test button all use "Connections" now. The internal tab id `bridge`, the `tbridge` element id, function names like `loadBridge`/`bridgeRefreshStatus`, and all source comments are intentionally NOT renamed ŌĆö same scope rule as v1.0.45's "Bridge ŌåÆ MT5 Connection" rename: user-visible surface only.

The Architecture-section docs prose ("server.py ŌĆö The bridge. Flask + SocketIO ...") is also unchanged ŌĆö it's an architectural metaphor in dev-facing docs, parallel to "engine.py ŌĆö The brain" and "dashboard.html ŌĆö The eyes", not a UI label.

### `release.ps1`: spaces ŌåÆ dots in the installer URL written to `latest.json`

`gh release upload` rewrites spaces in asset filenames to dots before they hit GitHub's CDN ("ASA Trading Systems Terminal_1.0.49_x64-setup.exe" arrives at `https://github.com/.../releases/download/v1.0.49/ASA.Trading.Systems.Terminal_1.0.49_x64-setup.exe`). The Tauri bundle filename is derived from `productName` which contains spaces, so the on-disk filename has spaces but the hosted asset URL has dots. Pre-v1.0.49 we baked the on-disk filename into `latest.json` and the Tauri auto-updater 404'd on every check ŌĆö v1.0.48 was caught and patched manually after publish; v1.0.49's first build re-introduced the bug because `release.ps1` itself was never patched.

`Get-InstallerUrl` now does `$InstallerFileName -replace ' ', '.'` before composing the URL, so the URL embedded in `latest.json` always matches the hosted asset.

### `release.ps1`: UTF-8 CHANGELOG read fixes manifest mojibake

v1.0.48's published `latest.json` displayed `1536├āŌĆö1024`, `├óŌé¼ŌĆØ`, `├óŌĆ░┬ż` etc. instead of `1536├Ś1024`, `ŌĆö`, `Ōēż`. PS5.1's `Get-Content -Raw` reads files as the system ANSI codepage (CP-1252 on US/UK Windows) by default, which decoded the UTF-8 em-dashes / `├Ś` / `Ōēż` bytes as Latin-1, producing two-byte mojibake when re-encoded as UTF-8 by `ConvertTo-Json` later.

Fixed by switching the changelog read to `[System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($changelogPath, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false))` ŌĆö explicit UTF-8 in, clean codepoints all the way to the JSON manifest. Same fix applied to the `latest.json` write (`Set-Content -Encoding utf8` adds a BOM on PS5.1; `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText` with `UTF8Encoding($false)` writes BOM-free).

### `release.ps1`: `git` push step shielded from PS5.1 NativeCommandError

`release.ps1` runs with `$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'`. PS5.1 wraps every line a native exe writes to stderr in a `NativeCommandError` object, and `Stop` treats the wrap as a *terminating* error ŌĆö even when the exe exited 0. `git clone` writes "Cloning into ..." to stderr by design, so the v1.0.48 manifest-push step (`git clone $repo` then `git push origin HEAD`) aborted before any commit happened on `main`. Asset upload had already finished, so v1.0.48 had a valid GitHub release but no main-branch manifest, and Fastly kept serving v1.0.47 from cache for the canonical updater URL.

Fix: every native-command call in step 8 now goes through `cmd.exe /c "<git ...> 2> $errFile"` and uses `$LASTEXITCODE` for success/failure. Stderr is captured to a file inside cmd.exe (where PS5.1 can't intercept it), then re-read and `Write-Host`'d so progress stays visible in the build log. `git commit` "nothing to commit" (exit 1) is now disambiguated from genuine failure by checking `git status --porcelain` before committing.

### "MT5 Connection" / "Bridge" ŌåÆ "Connections"

Sidebar nav button label, page heading, error-modal copy, and the onboarding broker-test button all use "Connections" now. The internal tab id `bridge`, the `tbridge` element id, function names like `loadBridge`/`bridgeRefreshStatus`, and all source comments are intentionally NOT renamed ŌĆö same scope rule as v1.0.45's "Bridge ŌåÆ MT5 Connection" rename: user-visible surface only.

The Architecture-section docs prose ("server.py ŌĆö The bridge. Flask + SocketIO ...") is also unchanged ŌĆö it's an architectural metaphor in dev-facing docs, parallel to "engine.py ŌĆö The brain" and "dashboard.html ŌĆö The eyes", not a UI label.

### Files touched

- `dashboard.html` ŌĆö sidebar nav button label, h2 heading, tsAlert error-modal copy
- `templates/onboarding.html` ŌĆö broker-test button + step-instruction copy
- `branding/svg/asa-glyph-s.svg` ŌĆö new ASA-current-greens SVG master, replaces v1.0.36 phosphor-green design
- `scripts/regen_icon.py` ŌĆö rewritten to render via resvg-py from the SVG (no raster downscale)
- `tray-app/src-tauri/icons/{32x32,64x64,128x128,128x128@2x,icon}.png`, `icon.ico` ŌĆö regenerated from the vector master at every DPI size
- `scripts/release.ps1` ŌĆö `Get-InstallerUrl` now rewrites spaces ŌåÆ dots; UTF-8 explicit on CHANGELOG read; UTF-8 BOM-free on `latest.json` write; `cmd.exe`-shielded `git clone`/`commit`/`push` in the manifest-push loop

### Auto-updater note

Same pipeline as v1.0.48. The freshly committed `latest.json` content (different bytes, different ETag) is what forces Fastly to re-fetch from origin, which is the actual mechanism by which an updater fix lands ŌĆö there's no GitHub API to flush raw.gh's CDN, but a content change reliably busts the cache key.
